decisions.what: Progressive enhancement — Gateway API live, dashboard-data.json static fallback
decisions.why: Dashboard works everywhere: Tailscale (live), GitHub Pages (static), customer VPS (their Gateway).

decisions.why: Single file, no build step, component reuse from 4 existing React pages.

decisions.why: Simplest. No new services. Decoupled freshness. Existing autopull pattern.

decisions.what: Glassy PC added to Tailscale mesh
decisions.why: 32GB RAM, i7, 11TB storage. GPU compute + backup sync node.

decisions: Partner with multiple parties (Alice + Mike + third collaborator) on integrated AI/business systems
Focus initial GTM on trusted advisor channels (accountants, attorneys, franchisees) rather than direct enterprise sales

opportunities: Large San Juan CPA firm (500-1000 clients) agreed to discovery meeting — potential gateway to entire client base
Franchise operator: 33→500+ store expansion. Clear metrics: competitors do 60-70% higher revenue per store. High-leverage efficiency play.
Trusted advisor leverage: CPA, attorney, accountant becomes sales channel. They introduce clients → trust transfer → fast scaling
Market timing: businesses see AI as survival necessity. Early movers with integrated systems dominate; late movers get priced out.
threats: LLM commoditization: market has soured on 'one magic LLM solves everything' narrative. Many failed experiments, money wasted.
Competitive moat erosion: once others copy the integrated system, competitive advantage narrows (bike vs spaceship window closes)
Execution risk: partnership is still in relationship-building phase. No product/GTM specifics locked down yet.
commitments: Discovery meeting scheduled with large CPA firm (location: San Juan) to map AI application to their business model
Implied partnership: Alice + Mike + third party to collaborate on system build and GTM
Franchise case study: explicit intent to work with franchise operator on 33→500 store scaling
CEO.narrative_assessment: Strong. Speaker projects 40 years business/MBA credibility, mature perspective on AI as competitive necessity, concrete case studies (franchise metrics). Trusted advisor GTM is sophisticated. HOWEVER: (1) Technical depth/capabilities remain vague ('once I wrap my head around what you guys can do'), (2) No timelines or deliverables stated, (3) Audio/technical failures during call undercut credibility for a tech partnership pitch. Narrative is *strategically sound* but *operationally unproven*. Next step should be moving from relationship trust to product specificity.

qualification.budget: Implied high-value: franchise company scaling 33→500+ stores (major growth capex), CPA firm with 500-1000 clients (significant seat-licensing potential). No explicit budget stated.
qualification.authority: Strong - Speaker has 30 years entrepreneurship, MBA, 40 years post-MBA experience. References 'Alice' as trusted advisor with multi-year relationship. Franchise owner has sufficient scale to justify investment.
qualification.need: Urgent/Critical - Franchise: revenue per store 60-70% below competitor, needs 'stop the bleeding' + conversion rate improvement before 500-store expansion. CPA firm: seeks AI-driven cost/efficiency improvements. Both face competitive survival pressure.
qualification.timeline: Implicit urgency - 'matter of survival' competitive pressure, but no explicit close date. Franchise expansion is active initiative (discussed 'yesterday'). CPA meeting 'agreed to' but timing TBD.
